front: Name the seven PMBOK 8 performance domains in any order.
back: Governance, Scope, Schedule, Finance, Stakeholders, Resources, Risk.

front: How do PMBOK 8 performance domains differ in design from PMBOK 7 domains?
back: 7th: 8 outcome-focused domains with no embedded processes. 8th: 7 function-oriented domains, each with processes and ITTOs (~40 total).
tip: 7th = what success looks like; 8th = what function you manage.

front: In PMBOK 8, which performance domain absorbed Integration, change control, knowledge management, quality assurance, and sourcing strategy?
back: Governance (9 processes). It is the integrative domain spanning Initiating through Closing Focus Areas.
tip: Governance ≈ Integration KA expanded + QA + strategic sourcing.

front: Which PMBOK 8 domain is the only one with processes in all five Focus Areas?
back: Governance. Scope, Schedule, Finance, Stakeholders, Resources, and Risk each span only two or three Focus Areas.

front: List the nine Governance domain processes (PMBOK 8).
back: Initiate Project or Phase; Integrate and Align Project Plans; Plan Sourcing Strategy; Manage Project Execution; Manage Project Knowledge; Manage Quality Assurance; Monitor and Control Project Performance; Assess and Implement Changes; Close Project or Phase.
tip: Init → integrate plans + sourcing → execute/knowledge/QA → monitor + change → close.

front: Where does quality assurance vs quality control live in PMBOK 8 domains?
back: QA (process audits, standards compliance) is Manage Quality Assurance under Governance. QC/deliverable inspection aligns with Scope (e.g., Validate Scope; Monitor and Control Scope).
tip: Process health → Governance; product fitness → Scope.

front: What does the Scope performance domain own in PMBOK 8, and about how many processes?
back: Defining and managing what is included: plan scope, elicit/analyze requirements, define scope, develop scope structure (WBS-like), validate scope, monitor/control scope (~6 processes; Planning + M&C).

front: What does the Schedule performance domain own, and how were classic schedule processes consolidated?
back: Planning and controlling the timeline (~3 processes: Plan Schedule Management, Develop Schedule, Monitor and Control Schedule). Define/Sequence/Estimate activities merged into Develop Schedule.
tip: Four old scheduling steps → one Develop Schedule process.

front: Why is Cost Management called Finance in PMBOK 8, and what does that domain cover?
back: Finance broadens Cost to funding, budgets, financial reporting, and value/financial performance—not only expense control. Typical processes: Plan Financial Management, Estimate Costs, Develop Budget, Monitor and Control Finances (~4).
tip: Exam trap: answer may say Finance, not Cost.

front: What moved into the Stakeholders performance domain that used to be a separate Knowledge Area?
back: Communications management (plan, manage, monitor communications) now sits with identify stakeholders, plan/manage/monitor engagement (~7 processes).
tip: No standalone Communications domain in PMBOK 8.

front: How does PMBOK 8 Resources relate to the 7th Edition Team domain?
back: Team content largely maps into Resources (people + physical assets). Develop Team + Manage Team merge into Lead the Team; broader culture themes also sit under the Empowered Culture principle.
tip: Team domain disappears as a domain; team leadership stays in Resources + principles.

front: What does the Risk performance domain own, and what happened to Uncertainty / dual risk analysis?
back: Risk replaces 7th’s Uncertainty domain with focused risk processes (~6). Qual + quant analysis merge into Perform Risk Analysis; ambiguity/complexity themes shift toward the Holistic View principle.
tip: Uncertainty → Risk; two analyses → one Perform Risk Analysis.

front: Map these 7th Edition domains: Planning, Measurement, Development Approach & Life Cycle — where did they go in PMBOK 8?
back: Planning: distributed into every domain’s planning processes. Measurement: distributed into each domain’s M&C (e.g., EVM in Finance). Dev Approach & Life Cycle: moved to The Standard (life cycles/Focus Areas), not a performance domain.

front: Map Project Work and Delivery from PMBOK 7 to PMBOK 8 domains.
back: Project Work → largely Governance (execution coordination, knowledge, change, close-out, sourcing). Delivery → largely Scope (requirements, structure, validation/acceptance).
tip: Work orchestration = Governance; what gets delivered = Scope.

front: Where does procurement live in PMBOK 8 relative to the seven domains?
back: Strategic make-or-buy/sourcing is Plan Sourcing Strategy in Governance. Detailed Conduct/Control Procurements are demoted to Appendix X4—not core domain processes.
tip: Strategy in Governance; tactics in the appendix.

front: Approximate process counts by PMBOK 8 domain (Governance through Risk) and total.
back: Governance 9, Scope 6, Schedule 3, Finance 4, Stakeholders 7, Resources 5, Risk 6 → ~40 processes embedded in domains (not the old 49 across 10 KAs).
tip: Governance is densest; Schedule is leanest.
